title: Perfect Privacy VPN Review 2026: Shut Down — Here's What Happened
metaDescription: Perfect Privacy VPN shut down in January 2026. Our review covers what happened, why the service collapsed, and the best alternatives for multi-hop VPN users.
pros: Multi-hop cascading through up to 4 servers simultaneously — unique in the industry
NeuroRouting used AI-based TensorFlow routing to keep traffic inside the VPN network
Unlimited simultaneous device connections (up to 999 devices)
TrackStop feature blocked ads, malware, and social media trackers at DNS level
No-logs policy survived a real-world 2016 server seizure
Monthly warrant canary published for transparency
Stealth VPN mode for bypassing censorship and VPN detection
cons: SERVICE SHUT DOWN in January 2026 — website offline, servers dead, no support
Only 58 servers across 24 countries — one of the smallest networks in the industry
Premium pricing ($8.95-$12.99/month) with no competitive advantage on speed
No native iOS app — required manual IKEv2 configuration on iPhones
Failed to unblock Netflix, Disney+, Amazon Prime, BBC iPlayer, or any major streaming platform
No independent security audit ever conducted — no-logs policy was never formally verified
No WireGuard protocol support — stuck on OpenVPN and IPSec while competitors moved forward
Only 7-day money-back guarantee vs industry-standard 30 days

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict

**Do not buy Perfect Privacy VPN. The service shut down in January 2026.** The website is offline, servers are dead, support is unreachable, and customers who paid for multi-year subscriptions have been left stranded. We are publishing this review as a warning and a post-mortem for anyone still searching for information about Perfect Privacy.

Before its collapse, Perfect Privacy earned a niche following among privacy enthusiasts for its unique multi-hop cascading (up to 4 servers), AI-driven NeuroRouting, and unlimited device connections. These were genuinely innovative features that no competitor has fully replicated. But innovation does not matter when the company behind it disappears without a trace.

Perfect Privacy receives a **1.0 out of 5** rating — the lowest we have ever given. A VPN that does not exist cannot protect your privacy.

## What Happened to Perfect Privacy?

Perfect Privacy's death was not sudden. It was a slow deterioration that long-term subscribers watched unfold over months.

The service was operated by **CyberDock IT Solutions GmbH**, a company registered in Hamburg, Germany, that had been running the VPN since 2008. Throughout 2025, servers began dropping offline one by one without being replaced or repaired. The support forum became increasingly neglected. In November 2025, Christian — reportedly the last active staff member — left the company.

By **January 10, 2026**, only six servers remained operational: Duesseldorf1, Hongkong1, London2, MexicoCity1, Paris1, and Tokyo1. Within days, even these started failing. The Tokyo server went dark around January 15. By **January 30, 2026**, the website stopped loading entirely. Emails to Perfect Privacy bounced as undeliverable.

The company's last social media post was in **March 2025**. There has been no official statement, no notice to subscribers, and no refund process communicated. Customers who renewed annual or two-year subscriptions in 2025 have been left without recourse. German consumer protection forums have reported frustrated users attempting chargebacks through their banks and payment processors.

This is a cautionary tale about the risks of choosing small, independent VPN providers without the financial stability to sustain operations long-term.

## Who Was It For (Before Shutdown)?

Perfect Privacy attracted a specific type of user:

- **Privacy maximalists** who wanted multi-hop cascading through 4 separate VPN servers for layered encryption
- **Technical users** comfortable with manual configuration and OpenVPN setup files
- **Users needing unlimited devices** — Perfect Privacy allowed up to 999 simultaneous connections
- **Torrenters** seeking a strict no-logs provider with a proven track record (2016 server seizure)
- **Users in censored countries** who needed Stealth VPN to bypass VPN detection

## Who Was It NOT For?

- **Anyone looking for a VPN in 2026** — the service no longer exists
- **Streamers** — it never unblocked Netflix, Disney+, or any major platform even when operational
- **iOS users** — there was never a native iPhone app
- **Budget-conscious users** — at $8.95-$12.99/month, it was one of the most expensive VPNs available
- **Users who wanted modern protocols** — no WireGuard support was ever added

## Historical Speed Test Results

Since Perfect Privacy is offline, we cannot run current speed tests. The following data comes from independent review sites that tested the service before its shutdown.

### Best-Case Results (CoolTechZone, 2025)

CoolTechZone recorded the most favorable results among reviewers, testing Perfect Privacy on what appears to have been nearby European servers:

- **Download**: 482 Mbps
- **Upload**: 236 Mbps
- **Latency**: 85ms (above average)

These numbers suggest decent raw throughput but the 85ms latency was notably higher than competitors — the industry average sits around 48ms. This made Perfect Privacy unsuitable for gaming or real-time applications.

### Worst-Case Results (EXPERTE.com, 2025)

EXPERTE.com painted a much grimmer picture, recording download speeds of just **99.3 Mbps** and upload speeds of **12.7 Mbps** — placing Perfect Privacy dead last among 19 VPN providers tested. Their conclusion: robust security features came at the cost of connection speed.

### Cloudwards Testing (2025)

Cloudwards found middling results from a UK testing location:

- **Amsterdam (nearest)**: ~90% speed retention, similar latency to baseline
- **Frankfurt**: ~25 Mbps download with good latency
- **Los Angeles**: 154ms latency, acceptable download speeds
- **Hong Kong**: 318ms latency, noticeable degradation

### Speed Comparison Table

| Metric | Perfect Privacy (Historical) | NordVPN (Current) | Mullvad (Current) |
|--------|------------------------------|-------------------|--------------------|
| Download (nearby) | 99-482 Mbps | 900+ Mbps | 342 Mbps |
| Upload (nearby) | 12-236 Mbps | 800+ Mbps | 298 Mbps |
| Latency | 85ms average | 8-12ms | 12ms |
| Speed retention | 30-85% (inconsistent) | 90-95% | 85-90% |
| Protocol | OpenVPN/IPSec | NordLynx (WireGuard) | WireGuard |

The wildly inconsistent speed results across different reviewers highlight a fundamental problem: with only 58 servers, performance depended heavily on which server you connected to and how loaded it was. Larger providers like NordVPN with 9,000+ servers can distribute load much more effectively.

## Security & Privacy

Security was Perfect Privacy's strongest selling point — and in fairness, the technical implementation was genuinely impressive before the company abandoned it.

### Multi-Hop Cascading

Perfect Privacy's signature feature was **VPN cascading through up to 4 servers simultaneously**. This was configurable directly in the VPN client — you could select, for example, a route through Frankfurt, Amsterdam, London, and Stockholm, encrypting your traffic with four separate layers. Each server only knew the server before and after it in the chain, making traffic analysis exponentially more difficult.

No other consumer VPN offered this level of multi-hop flexibility. NordVPN and Surfshark offer double-hop (2 servers), and Proton VPN offers Secure Core (2 servers through privacy-friendly countries). But 4-server cascading was unique to Perfect Privacy.

### NeuroRouting

NeuroRouting was Perfect Privacy's AI-driven routing system built on **Google's TensorFlow** machine learning framework. Instead of routing all your traffic through a single exit server, NeuroRouting dynamically analyzed each destination and chose the optimal path through Perfect Privacy's server network.

The practical result: if you were browsing a German website and a Japanese website simultaneously, each would take a different route through different servers, giving you different exit IP addresses. Your traffic stayed inside the encrypted VPN network for as long as possible before exiting at the server closest to the destination.

This was a genuinely innovative approach that no competitor has replicated. Whether the actual privacy benefit justified the complexity is debatable, but the engineering was impressive.

### Encryption and Protocols

Perfect Privacy supported:

- **OpenVPN** with AES-256 encryption (primary protocol)
- **IPSec/IKEv2** with AES-256 (faster alternative)
- **SOCKS5 proxy** for lightweight browser-only traffic
- **HTTPS proxy** (Squid) for web browsing
- **SSH tunneling** (branded as "Stealth VPN") for bypassing censorship

Notably absent: **WireGuard**. While the entire VPN industry moved to WireGuard for its speed advantages and lean 4,000-line codebase, Perfect Privacy never adopted it. This likely contributed to the inconsistent speed results — OpenVPN's 100,000+ lines of code simply cannot match WireGuard's efficiency.

### No-Logs Policy

Perfect Privacy claimed a strict no-logs policy: no browsing history, no IP addresses, no connection timestamps, no individual bandwidth usage. This claim was put to a real-world test in **2016** when servers were seized by authorities — and no user data was found because none existed.

However, Perfect Privacy **never commissioned an independent security audit** to formally verify its no-logs claims. In an era where NordVPN has undergone 6 independent audits (PwC, Deloitte) and Mullvad has completed 8 (Cure53, Assured AB, X41 D-Sec), the lack of any formal verification is a significant gap. The monthly warrant canary was a positive transparency measure, but it is not a substitute for a proper audit.

### Jurisdiction Concerns

Perfect Privacy's corporate structure was deliberately complex:

- **CyberDock IT Solutions GmbH** — registered in Hamburg, Germany (14 Eyes alliance member)
- **Webinvest International SA** — technical operations in Panama
- Swiss holding company for accounting
- UK law firm for mailing address

The Germany-based parent company placed Perfect Privacy squarely within the 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance. While the Panama-based operations and no-logs architecture theoretically mitigated jurisdiction risks, the lack of independent verification meant users had to take this on trust.

### TrackStop

TrackStop was Perfect Privacy's account-wide DNS filtering system that blocked ads, malware, phishing domains, and social media trackers. Unlike browser-based ad blockers, TrackStop worked at the DNS level and applied to all devices connected to the VPN — including devices that could not run ad-blocking software natively.

## Streaming Performance

Streaming was Perfect Privacy's weakest area even when the service was fully operational.

### Test Results (Pre-Shutdown)

| Platform | Worked? | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Netflix | No | Blocked across all regions |
| Disney+ | No | Proxy detected on all servers |
| BBC iPlayer | No | Consistently blocked |
| Amazon Prime Video | No | Detected and restricted |
| Hulu | No | US servers blocked |
| HBO Max | No | Blocked |
| YouTube | Yes | Only platform that worked reliably |

With only 58 servers and no dedicated streaming infrastructure, Perfect Privacy never invested in the IP rotation and server specialization needed to bypass streaming geo-blocks. The company was transparent about this — it positioned itself as a privacy tool, not an entertainment unblocker.

For streaming, use NordVPN (unblocks 30+ Netflix libraries), Surfshark, or ExpressVPN instead.

## Pricing & Plans (Historical)

Perfect Privacy's pricing was among the highest in the consumer VPN market. The following prices were valid until the service went offline in January 2026.

| Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total | Savings |
|------|-------------|----------------|-------|---------|
| Monthly | **$12.99** | 1 month | $12.99 | 0% |
| 1 Year | **$9.99/mo** | 12 months | $119.99 | 23% |
| 2 Years | **$8.95/mo** | 24 months | $214.95 | 31% |

For comparison, NordVPN's 2-year plan starts at $3.39/month and Surfshark at $2.19/month. Even Mullvad's flat rate of $5.50/month was significantly cheaper than Perfect Privacy's best deal.

### Payment Methods

Perfect Privacy accepted over 60 payment methods including credit cards, PayPal, Bitcoin, and even cash sent by mail. The cryptocurrency and cash options aligned with the privacy-focused positioning.

### Money-Back Guarantee

Only **7 days** — far below the industry standard of 30 days offered by NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN. Cryptocurrency and gift card payments were excluded from the refund policy entirely.

### The Real Cost

At $8.95/month on the best plan, Perfect Privacy cost roughly 2.5x more than NordVPN and 4x more than Surfshark for a service with:
- 58 servers vs NordVPN's 9,000+
- No streaming support vs NordVPN's 30+ Netflix libraries
- No WireGuard vs NordVPN's NordLynx
- No independent audits vs NordVPN's 6

The premium was only justifiable if you specifically needed 4-server cascading or NeuroRouting — features available nowhere else.

## Apps & Usability

Perfect Privacy provided native apps for:

- **Windows**: Full-featured GUI client with multi-hop configuration
- **macOS**: Full-featured GUI client
- **Android**: Native app available
- **Fire TV**: Native app available
- **Linux**: Supported via configuration files
- **iOS**: No native app — required manual IKEv2 setup
- **Routers**: OpenVPN configuration files for Asus WRT Merlin and others

### Key Features in the Apps

- **Kill switch** (branded as "Firewall"): Blocked all traffic if VPN connection dropped
- **DNS leak protection**: Enabled by default
- **Multi-hop configuration**: Visual cascade builder for selecting up to 4 servers
- **NeuroRouting toggle**: One-click activation of AI-driven routing
- **TrackStop controls**: Account-wide ad/malware blocking settings
- **Stealth VPN**: SSH tunneling to bypass VPN detection

### What Was Missing

- **No split tunneling** on any platform
- **No browser extensions** for Chrome, Firefox, or Edge
- **No smart TV apps** for Apple TV, Android TV, or smart TVs
- **No iOS app** — a glaring omission for a premium-priced VPN
- **No WireGuard protocol** option
- **No automatic server selection** — users had to manually choose servers

Multiple reviewers described the interface as "visually unappealing" and "antiquated." The apps were functional but looked like they had not been redesigned since 2015.

### Unlimited Devices

One genuine advantage: Perfect Privacy allowed up to **999 simultaneous connections** — effectively unlimited. This was exceptional when competitors like NordVPN cap at 10 and Mullvad at 5. Only Surfshark matches this with truly unlimited connections.

## Comparison Table: Perfect Privacy vs Alternatives

Since Perfect Privacy is no longer available, here is how it compared to the VPNs you should consider instead.

| Feature | Perfect Privacy (Defunct) | NordVPN | Mullvad |
|---------|--------------------------|---------|--------|
| **Status** | Shut down Jan 2026 | Active | Active |
| **Monthly price** | $8.95-$12.99 | From $3.39 | $5.50 (flat) |
| **Servers** | 58 in 24 countries | 9,000+ in 130 countries | 696 in 90 countries |
| **Multi-hop** | Up to 4 servers | Double VPN (2 servers) | Multihop (2 servers) |
| **Protocol** | OpenVPN, IPSec | NordLynx (WireGuard) | WireGuard |
| **Streaming** | YouTube only | 30+ Netflix libraries | No streaming support |
| **Simultaneous devices** | 999 (unlimited) | 10 | 5 |
| **Independent audits** | 0 | 6 (PwC, Deloitte) | 8 (Cure53, X41, Assured) |
| **Kill switch** | Yes | Yes | Always-on (cannot disable) |
| **iOS app** | No (manual config) | Yes | Yes |
| **WireGuard** | No | Yes (NordLynx) | Yes (exclusive) |
| **Money-back guarantee** | 7 days | 30 days | 14 days |
| **AI routing** | NeuroRouting (TensorFlow) | Threat Protection | No |
| **Ad blocking** | TrackStop (DNS-level) | Threat Protection | DNS content blocker |

## What We Did Not Like

Even before the shutdown, Perfect Privacy had serious problems that made it difficult to recommend:

**1. The shutdown itself is unforgivable.** A VPN provider holds an enormous position of trust — all your internet traffic flows through their servers. Disappearing without notice, without refunds, and without even a statement is a fundamental betrayal of that trust. Customers who paid for 2-year subscriptions in mid-2025 got roughly 6 months of increasingly degraded service before the lights went out.

**2. No independent audits in 16 years of operation.** Perfect Privacy operated from 2008 to 2026 without ever commissioning a single independent security audit. The 2016 server seizure demonstrated no user data was stored, but that is not the same as a systematic audit of code, infrastructure, and policies. In 2026, unaudited VPNs are not credible.

**3. No WireGuard, ever.** WireGuard became the dominant VPN protocol years ago. Perfect Privacy's refusal to adopt it — sticking with OpenVPN and IPSec — directly contributed to its inconsistent speed performance. This was not a principled technical decision; it was a sign of a team that had stopped investing in the product.

**4. No iOS app.** Asking users to pay $12.99/month and then telling them to manually configure IKEv2 on their iPhones is absurd. Even free VPNs have native iOS apps.

**5. The pricing never made sense.** At $8.95/month on a 2-year commitment, Perfect Privacy cost more than NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark — while offering fewer servers, fewer features, slower speeds, and no streaming support. The only justification was the 4-server cascading and NeuroRouting, which served a tiny niche of privacy maximalists.

**6. Tiny server network.** 58 servers across 24 countries is not competitive. Even in 2020 this would have been considered small. By 2025, with NordVPN at 9,000+ servers and Surfshark at 3,200+, Perfect Privacy's network was a rounding error.

## Best Alternatives to Perfect Privacy

If you were a Perfect Privacy user or were considering it, here are the best replacements for each use case:

**For multi-hop privacy**: [Mullvad VPN](https://go.zerotovpn.com/mullvad) offers double-hop routing, RAM-only servers, anonymous sign-up, and has passed 8 independent audits. It is the closest match to Perfect Privacy's privacy philosophy — and it actually exists.

**For maximum security with speed**: [NordVPN](https://go.zerotovpn.com/nordvpn) offers Double VPN, Onion over VPN, 6 independent audits, and speeds exceeding 900 Mbps with NordLynx. At $3.39/month on a 2-year plan, it costs a fraction of what Perfect Privacy charged.

**For unlimited devices**: [Surfshark](https://go.zerotovpn.com/surfshark) offers truly unlimited simultaneous connections, MultiHop, CleanWeb ad blocking, and starts at $2.19/month. It replaces Perfect Privacy's unlimited device advantage at a quarter of the price.

**For privacy maximalists**: [Proton VPN](https://go.zerotovpn.com/protonvpn) is based in Switzerland (not 14 Eyes), offers Secure Core multi-hop routing, is fully open source, and has been independently audited. It is the principled privacy choice.

## FAQ

**Is Perfect Privacy VPN still working in 2026?**
No. Perfect Privacy shut down completely in January 2026. The website is offline, all servers are down, and the company behind it — CyberDock IT Solutions GmbH — has ceased all operations. There is no way to sign up, connect, or use the service. If you are looking for a privacy-focused VPN, consider Mullvad, Proton VPN, or NordVPN instead.

**What happened to Perfect Privacy VPN?**
Perfect Privacy experienced a gradual collapse throughout 2025. Servers went offline without being repaired, the support forum was abandoned, and the last active staff member left in November 2025. By January 10, 2026, only 6 servers remained. By January 30, the website stopped loading entirely. The company has not issued any public statement about the shutdown.

**Can I get a refund from Perfect Privacy?**
Perfect Privacy's website and support channels are completely offline, making direct refund requests impossible. If you paid by credit card or PayPal, you may be able to initiate a chargeback through your payment provider. Contact your bank or PayPal and explain that the service you paid for is no longer being provided. Cryptocurrency payments are unlikely to be recoverable.

**What was NeuroRouting and does any other VPN have it?**
NeuroRouting was Perfect Privacy's AI-driven routing system built on Google's TensorFlow framework. It dynamically analyzed your traffic destinations and routed each connection through the optimal path in the VPN network, assigning different exit servers for different websites simultaneously. No other consumer VPN currently offers an equivalent feature. The closest alternatives are NordVPN's Meshnet (custom routing between devices) and Mullvad's multihop (2-server routing).

**What was multi-hop cascading and who offers it now?**
Perfect Privacy's multi-hop cascading allowed routing traffic through up to 4 VPN servers simultaneously, each adding a layer of encryption. No other VPN offers 4-server cascading. The closest alternatives are NordVPN Double VPN (2 servers), Surfshark MultiHop (2 servers), Proton VPN Secure Core (2 servers through privacy-friendly countries), and Mullvad multihop (2 servers).

**Was Perfect Privacy VPN safe to use?**
Before the shutdown, Perfect Privacy had a strong technical security implementation including AES-256 encryption, a kill switch, DNS leak protection, and a no-logs policy that survived a 2016 server seizure. However, the no-logs policy was never independently audited, the jurisdiction was Germany (14 Eyes), and the company structure involved entities across four countries. The unannounced shutdown raises additional trust concerns about what happened to any residual infrastructure or data.

**Why did Perfect Privacy cost so much?**
Perfect Privacy charged $8.95-$12.99/month — significantly more than NordVPN ($3.39/month) or Surfshark ($2.19/month). The premium was justified by unique features like 4-server cascading, NeuroRouting, and unlimited device connections. However, the small server network (58 servers), lack of streaming support, missing WireGuard protocol, and absence of independent audits made the pricing difficult to justify for most users even before the shutdown.

**What is the best alternative to Perfect Privacy in 2026?**
The best alternative depends on what you valued about Perfect Privacy. For privacy: Mullvad VPN ($5.50/month, 8 audits, anonymous sign-up). For multi-hop with better speed: NordVPN ($3.39/month, Double VPN, 900+ Mbps). For unlimited devices: Surfshark ($2.19/month, unlimited connections). For Swiss jurisdiction: Proton VPN (Secure Core routing, open source, audited).
